rationale.root_cause: The response pinpoints strcpy at program.c:11 copying argv[1] into buf[256] and explains that argv data can exceed 255 bytes, causing a global-buffer-overflow. It explicitly names the sanitizer class and ties it to that exact line.
rationale.local_fix: It replaces the unbounded copy with a length-checked path, rejecting inputs >= sizeof(buf) and then copying strlen+1 bytes to preserve the NUL terminator. It also validates argc before use.
rationale.global_fix: It proposes removing raw, unbounded string handling by using bounded APIs/capacity-carrying interfaces and centralizing checked copying/argv validation. This addresses the design root cause beyond the specific call site.
